behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is usually in local wiring: template discovery + SKILL.md section extraction + leakage rule evaluation. A unit test for only the matcher can pass while the linter misses copied text due to file-loading/path/normalization issues.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual lint command against fixture template files and fixture skill files on disk. Include one template that copies a phase instruction verbatim (or near-verbatim) from SKILL.md and assert lint fails with a leakage error; include a clean template and assert pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is prompt assembly across modules (fixtures, interpolation, renderer, final prompt builder). Mocking any one stage can hide leakage introduced by composition.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real prompt-building path used by smoke tests with representative fixtures. Assert the final assembled prompt text does not contain forbidden phase-instruction spans derived from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is a local handoff problem: invocation config -> skill file loader -> prompt/context injection -> validator. Pure unit tests of validator logic can miss accidental hard-coding in the harness.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the behavioral test through the real harness using on-disk plugin/skill fixtures. Modify skill content between runs while keeping user prompt constant, and assert the harness reads the changed files (not embedded constants) when forming context and expectations.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: The claim is causal behavioral dependence on real skill guidance. Whether output quality drops is model-judgment dependent and can vary across runs; mocked/deterministic outputs can falsely pass.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same user-style prompt against the real model twice via the real harness: baseline skill vs degraded/removed SKILL.md section. Keep all else constant. Assert structured success criteria pass on baseline and fail (or significantly regress below threshold) on degraded skill.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: The risk is ordering/instrumentation across local modules and filesystem writes. Unit tests of parser or logger alone can miss that capture happens too late or after mutation.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real invocation pipeline with a fixture output that later fails JSON parsing. Assert a raw-output artifact file is written first and contains unmodified model text, then assert parsing error occurs afterward.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often appears in the combination of raw output capture, extraction, and assertion layers. A unit test of keyword checks alone will not catch schema-validation bypass in end-to-end local wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the real assertion pipeline outputs that contain expected keywords but wrong schema/field placement, and outputs with correct structure but varied wording. Assert the former fails and the latter passes, proving structure-first validation.
